title: Opportunity
content:
The Digital Heart Lab (DHlab) in the Department of Biomedical Engineering,
National University of Singapore (NUS) has multiple openings for Postdoc, PhD
students, and visitors. Our lab is committed to interdisciplinary research at
the intersection of AI, cardiac modeling, and medical image analysis.

About Us

DHlab is affiliated in the NUS, which is an internationally renowned research
university, ranked 8th in the QS World University Rankings 2025. We aim to
develop advanced AI models for creating cardiac digital twins, i.e.,
personalized virtual heart models. We will employ the dataset (imaging, ECG,
electronic medical records, etc) collected from patients, to accurately
modelling the anatomy and simulate the function of patients' diseased hearts.
These models, coupled with machine learning techniques, contribute to the
identification of crucial mechanistic relationships and features that offer
insights into the trajectory of a patient's heart condition. The research will
delve into the intersection of AI and cardiac sciences, exploring novel
approaches to revolutionize our understanding of the human heart. With the
potential to impact medical treatments and technology advancements, it promises
an exciting and important avenue for personalized medicine. We will collaborate
with a multi-disciplinary team, including academics/ experts from NUS,
University of Oxford, Peking University, Imperial College London, Fudan
University, etc.

Research Areas

 * Medical Image Analysis
   
   * Aim: create 3D(+t) anatomical model from medical imaging.
   * Tech: image segmentation, motion extraction, mesh reconstruction, etc.

 * Electrophysiological Modelling
   
   * Aim: simulate and inverse infer the electrical activity in the heart.
   * Tech: mathematics modelling, signal processing, inverse inference, etc.

 * Machine Learning
   
   * Aim: establish AI models to advance cardiac modelling and simulation.
   * Tech: multi-modal AI, neural surrogate model, physics-informed model, etc.

 * Cardiac Digital Twin Application
   
   * Aim: employ the cardiac digital twins to assiss the personalized diagnosis
     and treatment of atrial fibrillation (AF), myocardial infarction (MI),
     ventricle tachycardia (VT), etc.
   * Tech: clinical evaluation, system development, interactive system, etc

Postdoc Opening Details

 * Requirement
   
   * Hold a PhD degree in biomedical engineering, computer science, data
     science, applied statistics/ mathematics, or any-related field.
   * Strong publication record and related research experience.
   * Candidates with cardiac modeling and simulation experience are preferred.

 * Salary
   
   * The competitive salary (~5500 SGD per month) is provided with NUS benefits,
     which can be discussed if you are exceptionally excellent.

 * How To Apply
   
   * Please send your CV, 1-2 page research statement, papers, and any other
     supporting materials, to lilei.sky@outlook.com. Email subject can be
     "Postdoc Application - Your University - Your Name".
   * 🌟 Please fill out this brief DHlab Postdoc Application Form, so I will not
     miss your application.
   * If your application materials are a good fit, I will reach out to you for a
     formal interview.

PhD Opening Details

 * Requirement
   
   * Being self-motivated and enthusiastic about AI for healthcare.
   * Have a good first degree in biomedical engineering, computer science, data
     science, applied statistics/ mathematics, or any-related field.
   * Strong problem-solving abilities and learning capability, such as top xx%,
     ranking xx out of xx, in your previous studies.
   * Proficiency in programming (Python, C++, etc) and in English reading &
     writing (requirements made by NUS: TOEFL/ IELTS/ GRE)
   * First-authored relative publication in top-tier journals and conferences
     are preferred.

 * Funding
   
   * Full PhD scholarships are available in the DHlab.
   * For excellent students, we will also recommend you to apply for other
     fundings, such as:
     * AISG PhD Fellowship
     * President's Graduate Fellowship
   * Furthermore, you can see fundings from others, such as:
     * A*STAR Singapore International Graduate Award (SINGA)
     * CSC Scholarship (only for visiting students from China)

 * How To Apply
   
   * Please send your CV, 1 page research statement, undergraduate transcripts
     (in English), and any other supporting materials, to lilei.sky@outlook.com.
     Email subject can be "PhD Application - Your University - Your Name".
   * 🌟 Please fill out this brief DHlab PhD Application Form, so I will not
     miss your application.
   * If your application materials are a good fit, I will reach out to you and
     encourage you to apply online in the NUS application system. Here are a few
     tips:
     * Apply for a PhD program rather than EngD.
     * For the source of finance, choose the NUS Research Scholarship or
       indicate any other funding you may have.
     * Be sure to select me as your proposed supervisor.
   * If you are a Singaporean/ PR, or you are willing to undertake a 3-year
     service obligation, i.e. work in Singapore after graduation, the admission
     bar may be slightly lower.

Other Research Positions Available

We are also welcome visiting students in our lab.

 * Application Process: Please refer to the instructions in the PhD section
   above for guidance on how to apply.

 * Internship Information:
   
   * NUS BS/MS/FYP Students: You are encouraged to register for research courses
     to demonstrate your commitment. Please note that typically, no funding is
     provided for these positions.
   * External BS/MS Students: Internship opportunities may be available for
     exceptionally outstanding candidates who express a strong interest in
     pursuing a PhD with us in the future.

Given the high volume of emails I receive, I may not be able to respond to all
applicants. Please understand that this does not reflect on your qualifications
or potential. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Biography#

Mr Fan Yang currently is a visiting PhD student in DHlab of NUS, supervised by
Dr. Lei Li. He received the master degree from Sichuan University (Chengdu,
China) in 2022, where he is currently pursuing the PhD degree with the College
of Computer Science. His research interests include multimodal learning,
information fusion, and computer vision.


Honors & Awards#

 * 2024: Outstanding postgraduate student of Sichuan University
 * 2024: Scholarship from the China Scholarship Council (CSC)
 * 2024: Huawei Scholarship (2022-2023)
 * 2022: Outstanding postgraduate student of Sichuan University
 * 2020: The second prize of the 16th China Graduate Mathematical Modeling
   Competition
 * 2020: The third prize of the 2nd China Graduate Artificial Intelligence
   Innovation Competition
 * 2020: The third prize of the 7th China Graduate Student Future Aircraft
   Innovation Competition


Academic Services#

 * Reviewer:
   * IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems (TNNLS)
   * IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology (TCSVT)
   * IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing (TGRS)
   * IEEE Internet of Things Journal (IOTJ)
   * Pattern Recognition (PR)
   * Knowledge-based Systems (KBS)
   * Neurocomputing
   * Computers, Materials & Continua
   * European Journal of Medical Research


Selected Publication#

 1. Fan Yang, Wei Li, Menglong Yang, Binbin Liang, and Jianwei Zhang:
    Multi-modal Disordered Representation Learning Network for Description-based
    Person Search, AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI), 38(15):
    16316-16324, 2024.
 2. Fan Yang, Binbin Liang, Wei Li, and Jianwei Zhang: Multidimensional Fusion
    Network for Multispectral Object Detection, IEEE Transactions on Circuits
    and Systems for Video Technology, 2024.
 3. Fan Yang, Wei Li, Binbin Liang, and Jianwei Zhang: Spatiotemporal
    Interaction Transformer Network for Video-Based Person Reidentification in
    Internet of Things, IEEE Internet of Things Journal, 10(14): 12537-12547,
    2023.
 4. Fan Yang, Xiangtong Wang, Xuan Zhu, Binbin Liang, and Wei Li: Relation-based
    global-partial feature learning network for video-based person
    re-identification, Neurocomputing, 488: 424-435, 2022.
 5. Fan Yang, Wei Li, Binbin Liang, Songchen Han, and Xuan Zhu: Multi-stage
    attention network for video-based person re-identification, in IET Computer
    Vision, 16(5): 445-455, 2022.

Biography#

Mr Guang Yang currently is a DPhil student in University of Oxford (Oxford, UK),
co-supervised by Prof Vicente Grau and Dr Lei Li. He obtained his bachelor
degree in Department of Engineering, King's College London (London, UK) in 2022.
Later, he received his master degree from Department of Electronic and
Electrical Engineering, University College London (London, UK) in 2023. His
research interest is AI for cardiac analysis, specifically focusing on medical
image analysis, multi-modal deep learning.


Research/ Education Experience#

 * 10/2023-now DPhil, Institute of Biomedical Engineering, University of Oxford,
   Oxford, UK
 * 09/2022-09-2023 Master, Department of Electronic and Electrical Engineering,
   University College London, London, UK
 * 06/2022-10/2022 Research Assistant, Department of Engineering, King's College
   London, London, UK
 * 09/2019-06/2022 Bachelor, Department of Engineering, King's College London,
   London, UK


Honors & Awards#

 * 2022: King's Undergraduate Research Fellowship
 * 2022: ENMS 2022 Distinguished Graduates


Selected Publication#

 1. Guang Yang, Jingkun Chen, Xicheng Sheng, Shan Yang, Xiahai Zhuang, Betty
    Raman, Lei Li, Vicente Grau: Contrast-Free Myocardial Scar Segmentation in
    Cine MRI using Motion and Texture Fusion, IEEE International Symposium on
    Biomedical Imaging (ISBI), 2025.

Hozen Li is a dedicated web developer and a volunteer responsible for developing
and managing our organization's website. He provides technical support to the
team.

Biography#

Dr. Lei Li is an Assistant Professor of National University of Singapore (NUS).
Previously, she was a Lecturer at University of Southampton and a Postdoc
University of Oxford. She received her Ph.D. degree in Shanghai Jiao Tong
University (SJTU) in 2021 and obtained the SJTU 2021 Outstanding Doctoral
Graduate Development Scholarship. She has been selected as the Rising Star of
Women in Engineering by Asian Deans' Forum 2023 and Featured Women in Science by
RSIP Vision. She serves as Board Member of SIG-Cardiac and Women in MICCAI (WiM)
and Area Chair of MICCAI 2024. Her research interest is AI for healthcare,
specifically focusing on cardiac digital twins, medical imaging, and multi-modal
AI.


Research/ Education Experience#

 * 10/2024-now Assistant Professor, Department of Biomedical Engineering,
   National University of Singapore, Singapore
 * 01/2024-10/2024 Lecturer, School of Electronics & Computer Science,
   University of Southampton, Southampton, UK
 * 08/2021-12/2023 Postdoctoral Research Assistant, Institute of Biomedical
   Engineering, University of Oxford, Oxford, UK
 * 09/2016-06/2021 PhD, School of Biomedical Engineering, Shanghai Jiao Tong
   University, Shanghai, China
 * 09/2012-06/2016 Bachelor, Department of Medical Information Engineering,
   Sichuan University, Chengdu, China


Honors & Awards#

 * 2024: IEEE TMI Distinguished Reviewer - Gold Level (2023-2024)
 * 2024: 2nd Prize of Shanghai Science and Technology Award (Natural Science
   Award)
 * 2024: Featured Woman in Science at RSIP Vision
 * 2023: The Rising Star of Women in Engineering - Asian Deans' Forum 2023
 * 2023: IEEE TMI Distinguished Reviewer - Gold Level (2022-2023)
 * 2022: SJTU 2021 Outstanding Doctoral Graduate Development Scholarship


Teaching#

 * 2025-now: Medical Imaging Systems (BN5207), NUS


Selected Academic Services#

 * Editor:
   * Guest Associate Editor: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging (TMI)
   * Guest Editor: Journal of Imaging
   * Editorial Board Member: Journal of Medical Artificial Intelligence
 * Board Member:
   * MICCAI Special Interest Group (SIG)-Cardiac
   * Women in MICCAI (WiM)
 * Area Chair:
   * MICCAI 2024
 * Organizer:
   * CARE 2024 Challenge: Comprehensive Analysis & computing of REal-world
     medical images
   * LAScarQS 2022 Challenge: Left Atrial and Scar Quantification & Segmentation
   * MyoPS 2020 Challenge: Myocardial Pathology Segmentation Combining
     Multi-sequence CMR
   * MS-CMRSeg 2019 Challenge: Multi-sequence Cardiac MR Segmentation
   * MM-WHS 2017 Challenge: Multi-modality Whole Heart Segmentation


Selected Publication#

 1.  L Li, et al. "Personalized topology-informed localization of standard
     12-Lead ECG electrode placement from incomplete cardiac MRIs for efficient
     cardiac digital twins." Medical Image Analysis (2025).
 2.  L Li, et al. "Solving the inverse problem of electrocardiography for
     cardiac digital twins: a survey." IEEE Reviews in Biomedical Engineering
     (2024).
 3.  L Li, et al. "Towards enabling cardiac digital twins of myocardial
     infarction using deep computational models for inverse inference." IEEE
     Transactions on Medical Imaging 43. no. 7 (2024): 2466-2478.
 4.  L Li, et al. "Multi-modality cardiac image computing: A survey." Medical
     Image Analysis 88 (2023): 102869.
 5.  L Li, et al. "MyoPS: A benchmark of myocardial pathology segmentation
     combining three-sequence cardiac magnetic resonance images." Medical Image
     Analysis 87 (2023): 102808.
 6.  L Li, et al. "Medical image analysis on left atrial LGE MRI for atrial
     fibrillation studies: a review." Medical Image Analysis 77 (2022): 102360.
 7.  L Li, et al. "AtrialJSQnet: a new framework for joint segmentation and
     quantification of left atrium and scars incorporating spatial and shape
     information." Medical Image Analysis 76 (2022): 102303.
 8.  L Li, et al. " AtrialGeneral: domain generalization for left atrial
     segmentation of multi-center LGE MRIs", MICCAI, 557-566, 2021.
 9.  L Li, et al. " Joint left atrial segmentation and scar quantification based
     on a DNN with spatial encoding and shape attention. " MICCAI, 118-127,
     2020.
 10. L Li, et al. "Atrial scar quantification via multi-scale CNN in the
     graph-cuts framework." Medical Image Analysis 60 (2020): 101595.

Miss Xiaoyue Liu is currently an undergraduate student at the Department of
Biomedical Engineering, NUS. She is participating in the CDE2605R Undergraduate
Research Program under the supervision of Dr. Lei Li. Her research focuses on
the intersection of deep learning and medical image analysis.

Miss Xicheng Sheng is currently a master student at the School of Data Science,
Fudan University (Shanghai, China), co-supervised by Prof. Xiahai Zhuang and Dr.
Lei Li. She obtained her B.S. degree in statistics from the School of
Management, Fudan University in 2022. She is passionate about exploring the
intersection of medical data analysis and healthcare innovation.

Mr Xingtao Lin currently is a PhD student in Fuzhou University (Fuzhou, China),
co-supervised by Prof. Xiahai Zhuang, Prof. Liqin Huang, Dr. Lei Li. He obtained
his master and bachelor degree in Fuzhou University (Fuzhou, China) in 2023 and
2019, seperately. His research interest is atrial fibrillation, with a primary
focus on improving the efficiency of diagnosis and treatment for this condition.
He is also concerned with how factors such as healthy eating, regular exercise,
and effective stress management impact heart health.

Mr Yang Zhang is currently a M.Sc student at the School of Data Science, Fudan
University (Shanghai, China), co-supervised by Prof. Xiahai Zhuang and Dr. Lei
Li. He obtained his B.S. degree in statistics from the School of Management,
Fudan University in 2023. His research interests focus on computer vision,
particularly in the medical image computing. He is also passionate about
programming and has a strong enthusiasm for the open-source community.

Mr Yilin Lyu is a PhD student at the Department of Biomedical Engineering, NUS,
supervised by Dr. Lei Li. He obtained his master and bachelor degrees in Dalian
University of Technology (Dalian, China) in 2024 and 2021, respectively. His
research interest is machine learning and digital twin.

Mr Zichen Jiang is currently an undergraduate student at the Department of
Computer Science, NUS. His research interest is AI for science and digital
twins.
